pr.title: Split out game mode files from gm_1B03
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Split the large game-mode translation unit gm_1B03.c into separate mode-focused files for Classic, Adventure, All-Star, and Multi-Man, with matching split ranges and build configuration updates. The PR mostly reorganized existing nonmatching and matched fragments, pruned gm_1B03.h prototypes into new headers, and made a few type/callback cleanups that produced a net decomp.dev report of 10 new matches, 9 corresponding broken matches from the old unit, and +160 matched bytes.

key_files.role: Split the old gm_1B03 address ranges into gm_1B03, gmclassic, gmadventure, gmallstar, and gmmultiman sections.
Added new NonMatching object entries for gmclassic.c, gmadventure.c, gmallstar.c, and gmmultiman.c.
Shrank the original unit, removed later mode blocks, and kept early gm_1B03 code plus minor typing/callback fixes.
New Adventure mode translation unit, including moved MinorScene data and many nonmatching function stubs from the old range.
New Classic mode translation unit, including moved scene table/function ranges and matched small functions.
New All-Star mode translation unit, including moved functions such as fn_801B5AA8 and gm_801B607C.
New Multi-Man mode translation unit, including moved functions such as gm_801B6320, gm_801B6808_OnLoad, gm_801B6834, gm_801B685C, and gm_801B69C0.
Pruned prototypes that now belong to the new mode-specific headers.
New Adventure header for moved public declarations.
New Classic header for moved public declarations.
New All-Star header for moved public declarations.
New Multi-Man header; included by gm_16AE.c and gmscdata.c for cross-unit declarations.
Added includes for the new game-mode headers so scene data references have declarations.
Added gmmultiman.h include for declarations used outside the new split unit.

what_changed: Replaced one broad gm_1B03 split entry with five smaller split entries: gm_1B03.c, gmclassic.c, gmadventure.c, gmallstar.c, and gmmultiman.c.
Added four new source files: gmadventure.c with 652 lines, gmallstar.c with 386 lines, gmclassic.c with 349 lines, and gmmultiman.c with 332 lines.
Reduced gm_1B03.c by moving most functions from roughly 0x801B2BA4 through 0x801B8AF8 out to mode-specific files.
Added four new headers for the split files and removed the corresponding long prototype block from gm_1B03.h.
Updated configure.py so the new split files are built as NonMatching objects after gm_1B03.c.
Updated gmscdata.c to include gmadventure.h, gmallstar.h, gmclassic.h, and gmmultiman.h.
Changed gm_80477738 from ResultsMatchInfo to MatchEnd in gm_1B03.c and assigned gm_80477738 directly from rmi->match_end in gm_801B1AD4.
Changed two UnkUnloadData callback assignments from fn_801B09F8 to fn_801B0A8C in gm_801B0A34 and gm_801B0AC8.
Typed gm_8048E230 and gm_8048E378 as CSSData and SSSData and passed their addresses in MinorScene initializers.
Removed an unnecessary cast in gm_801B2AF8 by assigning gm_801A5250() directly to a u8 pointer.
smart_moves: The split was reflected in all three required places: source files, headers, and config/GALE01/splits.txt address ranges.
The new files follow semantic game-mode names instead of keeping all logic in an address-named catchall gm_1B03.c.
Prototypes were moved out of gm_1B03.h rather than leaving the old header as a dumping ground after the split.
Cross-unit include fixes were kept local: gmscdata.c includes all newly split game-mode headers, while gm_16AE.c only adds gmmultiman.h.
The decomp.dev report shows the move preserved known matched snippets by reattributing them to their new units, with a small net +160 byte matching gain.
Using typed CSSData, SSSData, and MatchEnd declarations reduced placeholder usage and appears to have helped gm_801B1AD4 reach 100% in the bot report.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a large decomp unit, update splits.txt with exact text/data/bss/sdata/sbss/sdata2 boundaries for every new file, not just the build list.
Expect decomp.dev to report moved matched functions as broken in the old unit and new matches in the new unit; interpret this as relocation if the function names and byte counts line up.
After a file split, remove old prototypes from the source header and create mode-specific headers to avoid stale declarations and over-inclusion.
Address-named files like gm_1B03.c can be progressively reduced while mode names such as gmclassic, gmadventure, gmallstar, and gmmultiman make future work more searchable.

Better struct typing can be part of a split PR when it is directly needed for cross-unit declarations or matching, but the evidence here is mostly from the diff and bot report rather than reviewer discussion.
MinorScene data split-outs need careful handling of pointer-vs-object initializers, as shown by changing gm_8048E230 and gm_8048E378 uses to address-of expressions.
naming_conventions: New files use compact lowercase mode names under src/melee/gm/: gmclassic.c, gmadventure.c, gmallstar.c, and gmmultiman.c.
Header guards use MELEE_GM_CLASSIC_H, MELEE_GM_ALLSTAR_H, and MELEE_GM_MULTIMAN_H style names.
Existing address-derived function names such as gm_801B69C0 and fn_801B5AA8 were preserved during the split.
Lifecycle suffixes like _OnInit and _OnLoad were retained for functions such as gm_801B34B8_OnInit and gm_801B6808_OnLoad.
Split-file names in config/GALE01/splits.txt mirror the source paths, e.g. melee/gm/gmadventure.c and melee/gm/gmmultiman.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Created object boundaries at exact contiguous address ranges: gm_1B03 text 0x801B0348-0x801B2BA4, gmclassic 0x801B2BA4-0x801B3F40, gmadventure 0x801B3F40-0x801B5324, gmallstar 0x801B5324-0x801B6320, and gmmultiman 0x801B6320-0x801B8BB4.
Kept new objects marked NonMatching in configure.py while preserving matched small functions inside those new units.
Direct MatchEnd assignment in gm_801B1AD4 replaced the previous nested assignment/commented uncertainty and the bot reported gm_801B1AD4 improving from 85% to 100%.
Typed data symbols and address-of initializers for CSSData/SSSData helped express scene-table layout more accurately than UNK_T arrays.
Moved already-matching small functions into their correct new object ranges; decomp.dev listed corresponding broken matches in gm_1B03 and new matches in gmclassic, gmallstar, and gmmultiman.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 matched code stayed at 37.70% with +160 matched bytes.
Reported 10 new matches, including gm_801B69C0 in gmmultiman, gm_801B34B8_OnInit and gm_801B3D44 in gmclassic, gm_801B607C and fn_801B5AA8 in gmallstar, and gm_801B1AD4 in gm_1B03.
Reported 9 broken matches in the old gm_1B03 unit for functions that were moved to new units.
No human review comments or requested changes were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that make gmclassic.c, gmadventure.c, gmallstar.c, or gmmultiman.c Matching objects.
Search for follow-up renames of gm_801B2BA4 through gm_801B8AF8 after the gm_1B03 split.
Check whether gm_80477738, gm_8048E230, and gm_8048E378 received final symbol names or struct definitions.
Look for PRs that further split MinorScene tables by game mode or clean up gm_unsplit.h dependencies.
Compare decomp.dev bot reports around this PR to distinguish pure relocation matches from newly matched code.
